**4. Diagrams**

*   **Fig. 1: Optical Combustor**
    *   Components: natural gas, preheated air, particle seeding, window, exhaust
*   **Fig. 2: Experimental Setup for Particle Image Velocimetry (PIV)**
    *   Components: beam expansion (cylindrical lenses), convex lens, aperture, ICCD camera, PIV laser, filter (300-325 nm), combustor, video camera ,filter (532 nm), CCD camera for PIV

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   GT burner: G30 DLE (Dry Low Emissions)
*   Turbine range: SGT-100 (5 MW) to SGT-400 (13.4 MW)
*   Thermal power: up to 1 MW
*   Preheated air temperature: 400 °C
*   Particle size: 1 µm (titanium dioxide)
*   Pressure p: between 1.5 and 6 bars
*   Pressure drop Δp/p: between 1% and 3%

Here's the breakdown of the technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   f1 = -12.7 mm
*   f2 = 200 mm
*   f = 1000 mm
*   f = 50 mm
    *   f/5.6
*   Tpulse ≈ 8 ns
*   Epulse ≈ 4.6-5.5 mJ

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Figure 3: Experimental setup for the PLIF imaging
    *   Components and Labels:
        *   Concave cylindrical lens
        *   Convex Lens
        *   Aperture
        *   Beam Splitter
        *   Dye Cell
        *   ICCD Camera
        *   Filter
        *   Photo Multiplier
        *   Convex Lens
        *   Laser System
        *   Matrix Burner
        *   Combustor
        *   Video Camera
        *   ICCD Filter Camera

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*PIV Parameters:*

*   Laser pulse energy: 120 mJ
*   Laser pulse duration: 5 ns
*   Double-pulse delay: 10-15 µs
*   Repetition rate: 5 Hz
*   Camera resolution: 1376 x 1024 pixels
*   Interrogation window size: 12 x 12 pixels
*   Velocity accuracy: ±0.7 m/s
*   Subpixel accuracy: 0.1 pixel
*UV imaging parameters:*
*f=100 mm
*f/2

*PLIF Parameters:*

*   Dye laser: Lumonics HD-500
*   Nd:YAG laser: Quanta Ray, DCR-2
*   Filter wavelengths between 300 nm and 325 nm
*   Dye laser tuned to 283.55 nm

*Raman Parameters:*

*   Lasers: Spectra-Physics PIV-400
*   Wavelength: 532 nm
*   Pulse energy: 5 x 300 mJ
*   Pulse duration: 7 ns
*   Delay between pulses: ~50 ns
*   Max energy inside combustion chamber: 890 mJ/pulse
*   Spectrograph Acton Research SpectraPro,
*   f= 300 mm
*   Grating: 490 lines/mm, f/4
*   dispersion 6 nm/mm
*   Magnification of optics: 2
*   CCD: 1340 x 1300 pixels, 26.8 mm x 26 mm
*   1D volume cell diameter: ~0.5 mm
*   1D volume cell length: 0.25 mm
*   Number of cells: 28
*   Probing laser beam diameter: 7mm
*   Repetition rate: 10 Hz

title: 09_PIV_2D-LIF_and_1D-Raman_measurements_of_flow_field_composition_and_temperatur
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the image, structured as requested:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*  Δp/p = 1%
*  Φ= 0.59

**2. Graphs/charts**

*   **Figure 5:**
    *   **Axes:** Although not explicitly labeled, the plot appears to represent a 2D spatial domain of the flame.
    *   **Units:**  The absolute RMS of the planar flow velocity uses arbitrary units.
    *   **Curves:**  N/A, this is a vector field.
    *   **Key values:**
        *   max: Maximum absolute RMS of flow velocity (value unspecified)
        *   50%: Intermediate absolute RMS value
        *   0: Zero absolute RMS of flow velocity
        *   Vectors: show the average planar flow field direction and magnitude.  The background is grayscale, mapping the absolute standard deviation of the planar flow velocity.

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 4: Experimental setup for 1D laser Raman spectroscopy**
    *   Components and labels:
        *   Laser System.
        *   Beam expansion (3.3:1)
        *   Cylindrical lenses (convex, f=300 mm)
        *   Apochromatic lens (f~280 mm)
        *   ICCD Camera.
        *   Grating Spectrograph ( f=300 mm, 490 lines/mm)
        *   Notch filter(532 nm)
        *   Focus inside of combustor
        *   Convex lens
        *   Power meter

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   f = 300 mm (focal length of cylindrical lenses)
*   f ≈ 280 mm (focal length of apochromatic lens)
*   490 lines/mm (grating spectrograph)
*   532 nm (notch filter wavelength)
*   p = 3 bar (pressure)
*   Δp/p = 1% (relative pressure drop over burner)
*   Φ = 0.59 (equivalence ratio)
*   8% (relative standard deviation of single-shot temperature measurements)
*   0.5 cm to 2 cm (observed Vortex diameters), also 5cm

title: 09_PIV_2D-LIF_and_1D-Raman_measurements_of_flow_field_composition_and_temperatur
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   No explicit equations are present in the text or figures.

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 8:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: mixture fraction
        *   y-axis: temperature T [K]
        *   Top x-axis: equivalence ratio Φ
        *   Scales:
            *   Mixture fraction: 0.00 to 0.10
            *   Temperature: 0 to 2500 K
            *   Equivalence ratio: 0 to 1.8
    *   Curves:
        *   Green curve: Represents the calculated adiabatic temperature of the reaction products.
    *   Data Points:
        *   Blue points (dark gray in black and white print): measured in the inflow of the unburnt fuel/air mixture. XH2O > 0.

*   **Figure 9:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: mixture fraction
        *   y-axis: temperature T [K]
        *   Top x-axis: equivalence ratio Φ
        *   Scales:
            *   Mixture fraction: 0.00 to 0.10
            *   Temperature: 0 to 2500 K
            *   Equivalence ratio: 0 to 1.8
    *   Curves:
        *   Green curve: Represents the calculated adiabatic temperature of the reaction products.
    *   Data Points:
        *   Blue points (dark gray in black and white print): measured in the inner shear layer. XH2O = 0.
        *   Red points (light gray): Xfuel = 0.

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 7 (a) and (b):**
    *   Images of OH PLIF intensity distributions.
    *   Color scale: 0 to 50% (OH PLIF intensity)

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Operating Conditions (Figure 7):
    *   (a):
        *   p = 1.5 bar
        *   Δp/p = 1.1%
        *   Φ = 0.53
    *   (b):
        *   p = 4 bar
        *   Δp/p = 3%
        *   Φ = 0.50
*   Data points in Figures 8 & 9: 14,000 points
